title: Occasioned By Some Verses Of His Grace The Duke Of Buckingham
content:
Muse, 'tis enough: at length thy labour ends,
And thou shalt live, for Buckingham commends.
Let Crowds and Critics now my verse assail,
Let Dennis write, and nameless numbers rail:
This more than pays whole years of thankless pain;
Time, health, and fortune are not lost in vain.
Sheffield approves, consenting Phoebus bends,
And I and Malice from this hour are friends.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Alexander Pope delivers a powerful performance in "Occasioned By Some Verses Of His Grace The Duke Of Buckingham"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
